Title: Dragging from a choice handle
Summary: Each choice has its own handle. Drag from it to wire that specific choice to the next sequence.
Body:
## Per choice handles

On a sequence node, every choice gets a small filled square on the right edge of the node, vertically aligned to its choice row. That is the handle for connecting that specific choice to a target.

Lines that have no choices yet show a smaller dashed outline placeholder handle anchored to the line's text. Drag from the placeholder to start a brand new choice on that line.

## The connect gesture

Click and drag from a handle. While dragging, a preview line follows the cursor. Release on:

- An existing sequence node, and the choice gains a NextSequenceId pointing to that target.
- Empty canvas, which pops a prompt for a new sequence id, creates the sequence with one empty line in the active folder, wires this choice to it, and saves the layout position at the drop point. See [drag to empty canvas](/help/dialogs-and-the-graph/drag-to-empty) for the details.

Drop on the same node and nothing happens.

## Why a handle per choice

Earlier versions had one handle per line. With one handle per choice, the visual mapping is direct: every wire on the canvas matches a row in the node body, with no need to count down to the right one. It also makes choice colored connectors readable: edges, the per choice arrow glyph in the node body, and the handle squares all resolve through one color variable.
